settings.name: OKF_VAULT_PATH
settings.description: Directory used as the OKF bundle / Obsidian vault root.

help.source: The skill ships this safe default.
instructions: Keep the default for normal use, or override with OKF_VAULT_PATH.
Must NOT point inside the skill directory.

settings.name: OKF_ENRICH_PROVIDER
settings.description: LLM provider for ingestion enrichment (openai = any OpenAI-compatible API).

settings.name: OKF_ENRICH_MODEL
settings.description: Model id for enrichment; empty = provider default (e.g. gpt-4.1-mini).

settings.name: OKF_INDEX_AUTO_CONFIRM
settings.description: Allow trusted automation to skip mutation confirmation.

help.source: The skill ships this safe default.
instructions: Override with OKF_INDEX_AUTO_CONFIRM=1 only in trusted automation.

settings.name: OKF_BODY_MAX_BYTES
settings.description: Max body bytes indexed per concept (larger bodies are truncated).

instructions: Override with OKF_FTS_TOKENIZER only if you know the FTS5 grammar.

settings.name: ENRICH_API_KEY
settings.description: API key for the LLM enrichment provider (OpenAI-compatible protocol).

instructions: Use auth setup for local OS keyring storage.
Use ENRICH_API_KEY only as the CI/headless environment fallback.
Never put the value in chat, argv, settings.json, or repository files.

settings.name: ENRICH_BASE_URL
settings.description: Base URL for the OpenAI-compatible enrichment endpoint (empty = default).

instructions: Set ENRICH_BASE_URL if your provider has a custom endpoint.

settings.name: CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL
settings.description: Base URL of the Confluence instance (embedded client).

help.source: Set to your Confluence instance URL.

settings.name: CONFLUENCE_USERNAME
settings.description: Username for Confluence REST API (optional — leave empty for Bearer-token-only auth).

instructions: Use auth setup for local OS keyring storage.
Use CONFLUENCE_USERNAME only as the CI/headless environment fallback.

settings.name: CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN
settings.description: Bearer token for the Confluence REST API.

help.source: Create a personal access token in Confluence profile settings.
instructions: Use auth setup so terminal input is hidden and stored in the OS keyring.
Use CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN only as the CI/headless environment fallback.
Never put the value in chat, argv, settings.json, or repository files.
